title: Human Fracture Analysis Constraint and Scoring Logic
purpose: Defines how multiple structural signals are aggregated into an overall structural risk assessment.

combination_rules.name: Child Harm Escalation
combination_rules.logic: If FES-04 is present, minimum overall structural_risk_level = high. If FES-04 co-occurs with any of FES-02, FES-07, or FES-11, overall structural_risk_level = critical.

combination_rules.name: Collapse Before Moralization
combination_rules.logic: If FES-10 is present, system must output collapse_containment_priority = true. Structural evaluation may proceed, but stabilization has priority.

combination_rules.name: Coercion Cluster
combination_rules.logic: If FES-01 co-occurs with FES-07 or FES-08, raise dignity_assessment to failing unless strong protective evidence exists.

combination_rules.name: Bitterness Operationalized
combination_rules.logic: If FES-03 co-occurs with FES-02, FES-04, or FES-07, treat bitterness as operational and increase overall risk by one level.

combination_rules.name: Narrative Bias Warning
combination_rules.logic: If FES-11 is present without self-accountability markers, output narrative_bias_warning = strong.

combination_rules.name: Protective Signal Moderation
combination_rules.logic: Protective signals may soften explanation tone but cannot downgrade critical child or collapse risk.

combination_rules.name: Self-Erasure Escalation
combination_rules.logic: If FES-06 co-occurs with FES-10, output self_annihilation_risk = severe and prioritize containment.

scoring_algorithm.step_1: Assign points to each detected risk signal.
scoring_algorithm.step_2: Do not subtract protective signals mechanically.

baseline_thresholds.condition: One high or two moderate signals.
Multiple high signals or child/coercion patterns.
Child leverage cluster or collapse cluster.
